title: Documentation Standard
summary: Baseline structure for docs across the Rails apps so we can migrate content without rewriting it.

sections.heading: Why this format
body: We keep docs in a single JSON file so content stays portable while the data model stays flexible.
The schema mirrors the fields we have used across other apps, without forcing a database migration yet.

sections.heading: Required fields
bullets: Title and summary for index cards.
At least one section so every doc has a clear body.

sections.heading: Optional structure
body: Tags, audience, and status help filter docs later without changing the content format.
Sections can include paragraphs, bullets, steps, and code blocks as needed.

title: Access + Approval Gating
summary: Standard approach for who can view and edit content in admin dashboards.

sections.heading: Default policy
body: Public pages stay readable without authentication.
Create/edit actions require a signed-in user with approval or admin access.

sections.heading: Approval workflow
steps: Admin approves the user in the console or admin UI.
Approved users see the dashboard links and can manage content.

sections.heading: Notes
bullets: Nav links should disappear for unapproved users.
Destructive actions should be admin-only unless explicitly allowed.

title: Calendar Behavior Notes
summary: How we document calendar views so scheduling logic stays consistent across apps.

sections.heading: Month view
body: Month calendars should explain what each block represents (events, tasks, rentals).
Multi-day items should display on every day in the range, not only the start day.

sections.heading: Daily view
bullets: Show the selected date in the heading.
Display items in priority order when possible.
Include a short legend if colors or emojis encode meaning.

title: Media + Embeds
summary: Consistent rules for images, YouTube embeds, and other media fields in docs.

sections.heading: Images
body: Images can be listed as filenames or S3 keys depending on the app.
Keep images in a separate list so the body stays clean and exportable.

sections.heading: Embeds
bullets: YouTube IDs should be stored without full URLs.
Start time should be optional and stored as seconds.

title: S3 Media + Proxy Fallback
summary: Short standard note for apps that use S3 with an image proxy fallback.

sections.heading: Expected behavior
bullets: Prefer signed proxy URLs when proxy config is present.
Fallback to presigned S3 URLs when proxy config is missing or invalid.

sections.heading: Doc checklist
steps: Record the expected object key pattern.
Call out the fallback behavior explicitly.

title: Doc Search Indexing
summary: How docs JSON becomes searchable through embeddings.

sections.heading: Import flow
steps: Entries are flattened into a title + body string.
Records are stored in the documents table for embedding search.

title: Media Example
summary: Example entry showing how images and videos render on a doc page.

sections.heading: Images + Video
body: Media can be attached per section using images and videos arrays.
Images use local assets or remote URLs, while videos embed YouTube links.

images.caption: S3 key (fallbacks to local logo if not configured).

videos.caption: Rick roll demo for the YouTube embed.
